blockingIssues.description: `internal/paper/paper.go` allows `papers` and `authors` slices to serialize as `null`, breaking the stable machine-readable schema expected by unified search responses.
bioRxiv and medRxiv search are implemented as a last-30-days category feed lookup instead of real query search, so valid keyword/title/author searches and older matches are missed.
The OpenAIRE connector targets `/search/publications` and unconditionally decodes JSON even though the live endpoint returns XML by default, causing production searches to fail.
Explicitly requested gated sources such as IEEE/ACM are silently dropped from search instead of returning an explicit machine-readable gated/unsupported response.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented OpenAIRE's format-sensitive search behavior and the XML-first expectation for future workers.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit guidance to verify parity-sensitive provider behavior against the reference implementation or official provider docs before codifying tests around endpoint/query semantics.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The first-wave connector review found bioRxiv/medRxiv tests locking in a category-feed approximation rather than true query search behavior, and the worker transcript shows that provider-parity research had to be discovered ad hoc.
